insights: Luke Wilson has a 41% incident closure rate compared to his peers' average of 52.3%, representing an 11.3 percentage point gap that persists across all categories, priorities, locations, and caller types — confirming a systemic closure bottleneck rather than case-mix bias.

Luke Wilson’s incidents in the Resolved state are, on average, 1,429 hours old — 3.2 times older than peers’ median resolved incident age of 442 hours — indicating a workflow stall at the final closure step, not resolution execution.

100% of Luke Wilson’s 59 Resolved incidents remain assigned to him, with 23% stuck in Resolved state for over 2,000 hours, while peers show higher reassignment rates and shorter resolved-state dwell time — revealing a critical assignment governance gap.

All five agents handled exactly 100 incidents each, eliminating workload volume imbalance as an explanation for performance discrepancies and isolating the root cause to process execution and handoff discipline rather than capacity constraints.

Negative resolution times affect 52.8% of all incidents and worsen sharply over time (7.3% in Jan 2023 → 97.1% in Jan 2024), invalidating resolution_time_hours as a performance metric but leaving closure rate and resolved incident age robust and actionable for evaluation.

The Sales department (4 agents) can absorb Luke Wilson’s 59 unresolved Resolved incidents during absence by redistributing ~20 incidents per peer — well within their 125-incident operational buffer — enabling immediate, low-risk workload continuity planning.
summary: The analysis confirms Luke Wilson is the underperforming agent referenced in the goal, with a statistically and operationally significant 11.3-percentage-point closure rate gap (41% vs. 52.3% peers), driven not by slower resolution work or case complexity, but by incidents stalling in the Resolved state — averaging 1,429 hours old (3.2× peers) and remaining exclusively assigned to him. This reflects a breakdown in post-resolution handoff or closure governance, not capacity or skill limitations. Critically, resolution time metrics are invalidated by pervasive data quality issues (52.8% negative values, worsening over time), reinforcing closure rate and resolved incident age as the only reliable performance indicators. Given perfectly balanced incident volumes across all agents (100 each), the issue is purely procedural — not resourcing-related. For absence management, the Sales team has sufficient headroom to absorb Luke’s 59 pending Resolved incidents with minimal strain, supporting immediate implementation of structured redistribution protocols and monitoring of resolved-state dwell time as a leading indicator of closure health.
